title: LedgerCloseMeta Business Logic Specification
description: This specification defines the business logic constraints and invariants for LedgerCloseMeta, used to validate and generate test XDR for downstream consumers.

versionConstraints.description: Determines which LedgerCloseMeta version to use based on protocol version

v2.description: Used for protocol versions >= 23 (parallel Soroban phase)

hash.description: SHA256 hash of the LedgerHeader XDR
invariants: hash == SHA256(header)
ledgerVersion >= previousLedger.ledgerVersion
If upgrade applied: ledgerVersion may increase by 1 from previousLedger

previousLedgerHash.description: Hash of the previous ledger header
invariants: previousLedgerHash == txSet.previousLedgerHash (for v1/v2)
previousLedgerHash == txSet.previousLedgerHash (for v0)
previousLedgerHash == previousLedger.hash
txSetHash == SHA256(GeneralizedTransactionSet) for v1/v2
txSetHash == SHA256(TransactionSet) for v0
closeTime > previousLedger.closeTime
closeTime is reasonably close to wall-clock time
Each upgrade is a valid encoded LedgerUpgrade
Upgrades are sorted by LedgerUpgradeType
At most one entry per LedgerUpgradeType
txSetResultHash == SHA256(TransactionResultSet)
TransactionResultSet.results corresponds 1:1 with txProcessing entries
Order matches hash order of transactions in txSet
ledgerSeq == previousLedger.ledgerSeq + 1
totalCoins == previousLedger.totalCoins + minted - burned
Minting only happens during inflation (deprecated)
Burning happens from fees and failed operations
totalCoins <= 1000000000000000000 (100 billion XLM)
feePool >= 0
feePool increases by sum of all feeCharged in this ledger

baseFee.description: Base fee per operation in stroops
invariants: Can only change via LEDGER_UPGRADE_BASE_FEE upgrade
Can only change via LEDGER_UPGRADE_BASE_RESERVE upgrade

maxTxSetSize.description: Maximum number of operations in a transaction set
invariants: txSet.totalOps <= maxTxSetSize

skipList.description: Hashes of previous ledgers for quick traversal
invariants: skipList[0] = hash of ledger at (ledgerSeq - ledgerSeq % 50)
skipList[1] = hash of ledger at (ledgerSeq - ledgerSeq % 5000)
skipList[2] = hash of ledger at (ledgerSeq - ledgerSeq % 50000)
skipList[3] = hash of ledger at (ledgerSeq - ledgerSeq % 500000)

rules: txs are sorted in hash order
All transactions are valid for the ledger
phases[1] is the Soroban phase (ParallelTxsComponent for v1, v2)
Classic phase transactions are sorted by hash
Soroban phase contains parallel execution stages

invariants: txProcessing entries are in APPLY order, not hash order
Classic transactions come before Soroban transactions
Within phases, order matches the phase's internal ordering
txProcessing.length == total number of transactions in txSet
txProcessing.length == txResultSet.results.length
Always contains STATE change followed by UPDATED change for source account
Balance decreases by feeCharged amount
Sequence number increment happens in txChangesBefore, not here
For fee bump transactions, fee source is debited
For regular tx: hash of TransactionSignaturePayload
For fee bump inner tx: hash of inner TransactionSignaturePayload
Hash is computed over the envelope contents
feeCharged >= baseFee * numOperations
feeCharged <= tx.fee (declared fee)
feeCharged reflects actual fee paid
Contains sequence number bump for source account
STATE entry shows pre-bump state
UPDATED entry shows post-bump state with new seqNum
seqLedger and seqTime are updated to current ledger values
operations.length == transaction.operations.length
Each operation has associated LedgerEntryChanges
Changes follow STATE -> (CREATED|UPDATED|REMOVED|RESTORED) pattern
For Soroban ops (v4): includes ContractEvents
Usually empty for most transaction types
May contain cleanup or finalization changes
null for non-Soroban/classic transactions
NOT null for successful Soroban transactions (InvokeHostFunction, ExtendTTL, RestoreFootprint)
For v3: contains events, returnValue, diagnosticEvents
For v4 (SorobanTransactionMetaV2): returnValue is null for failed txs, or an SCVal for successful txs
events contains only CONTRACT type events
diagnosticEvents contains all diagnostic info including failed calls

v4Structure.ext.v1.totalNonRefundableResourceFeeChar: Fee portion that cannot be refunded
v4Structure.ext.v1.totalRefundableResourceFeeCharged: Fee portion that can be refunded
v4Structure.returnValue: SCVal returned by contract invocation (e.g. 'void' for no return value), or null for failed transactions

invariants: Classic transactions: exactly 1 event with stage='before_all_txs' (fee charge)
Soroban transactions: 2 events - 'before_all_txs' (fee charge) AND 'after_all_txs' (fee refund)
before_all_txs fee event: data.i128 is POSITIVE (fee charged from source)
after_all_txs fee event: data.i128 is NEGATIVE (refund to source)
Fee event contract_id is the native SAC contract ID (e.g. 'CDLZFC3SYJYDZT7K67VZ75HPJVIEUVNIXF47ZG2FB2RMQQVU2HHGCYSC')
Fee event topics: ['fee', <source_account_address>]
Empty [] for classic transactions
Populated for Soroban transactions with core_metrics, fn_call, fn_return events
Each event has in_successful_contract_call boolean flag
core_metrics events track: read_entry, write_entry, cpu_insn, mem_byte, etc.

postTxApplyFeeProcessing.description: V2 only: post-application fee changes for Soroban refunds
invariants: Only present in LedgerCloseMetaV2 (TransactionResultMetaV1)
Empty [] for classic transactions
Populated for Soroban transactions with STATE + UPDATED for source account
Contains the Soroban fee refund (balance increases by refund amount)
Refund amount = declaredFee - feeCharged

LEDGER_ENTRY_STATE.description: Captures the state of an entry before modification
invariants: Must be followed by UPDATED or REMOVED for the same key
Only emitted once per entry per operation
lastModifiedLedgerSeq reflects when entry was previously modified
Entry did not exist before this operation
lastModifiedLedgerSeq == current ledger seq
No STATE entry precedes this for the same key in this operation
Must be preceded by STATE entry for the same key
lastModifiedLedgerSeq == current ledger seq
Entry content differs from STATE entry
Must be preceded by STATE entry for the same key
Entry will not exist after this operation
Only applies to persistent Soroban entries
Entry was previously evicted/archived
lastModifiedLedgerSeq == current ledger seq

generalInvariants: Changes are ordered: STATE before UPDATED/REMOVED
Each key appears at most once as STATE in an operation
Created entries don't have preceding STATE
Balance changes are consistent (debits = credits + fees)

invariants: upgradesProcessing.length == scpValue.upgrades.length (for valid upgrades)
Invalid upgrades are skipped and not recorded
upgrade field contains the decoded LedgerUpgrade
changes contains CONFIG_SETTING modifications for config upgrades
Applied after all transactions, before finalizing ledger

totalByteSizeOfLiveSorobanState.description: Size in bytes of live Soroban state
invariants: Only present in v1/v2
Used by downstream systems for storage fee calculation
Value comes from SorobanNetworkConfig.getAverageSorobanStateSize()
Contains TTL keys and persistent data/code keys that were evicted
Temporary entries are deleted (not archived)
Persistent entries are archived to hot archive

ext.v1.sorobanFeeWrite1KB.description: Fee rate for writing 1KB of Soroban state
invariants: Only present when EMIT_LEDGER_CLOSE_META_EXT_V1 is true
Value comes from SorobanNetworkConfig.feeRent1KB()
contractID is the contract that emitted the event
For native asset SAC events, contractID is derived from Asset
topics array contains event discriminator
data contains event payload
Fee events have 'fee' as first topic symbol
Transfer events have 'transfer' as first topic symbol
Fee events are emitted as TransactionEvent with stage BEFORE_ALL_TXS
Fee event contractID is native SAC contract ID
Fee event topics: [symbol:'fee', address:feeSource]
Fee event data: i128 representing fee amount in stroops
One fee event per transaction
Sum of all account balances + feePool == totalCoins
Fee source balance decreases by exactly feeCharged
For successful payments: sender decreases, receiver increases by same amount
For failed transactions: only fee is deducted
Claimable balance amounts are accounted in totalCoins
After tx: sourceAccount.seqNum == tx.seqNum
seqLedger updated to current ledgerSeq
seqTime updated to current closeTime
For fee bump: inner tx seqNum is used
Sequence gaps allowed with minSeqNum precondition

testGenerationGuidelines.description: Guidelines for generating valid test LedgerCloseMeta
steps: 1. Determine protocol version and select appropriate LedgerCloseMeta version (v0/v1/v2)
2. Generate valid LedgerHeader with proper previousLedgerHash
3. Create transaction set with valid transactions
4. Compute transaction results based on transaction logic
5. Generate fee processing changes for each transaction
6. Generate operation-level changes following STATE->UPDATED/CREATED/REMOVED pattern
7. For Soroban: generate proper events and sorobanMeta
8. Apply any upgrades and record upgrade processing
9. Compute all hashes (header hash, txSetResultHash, etc.)

requires: Source account STATE->UPDATED (seqNum bump, balance decrease)
Source account STATE->UPDATED (balance decrease by starting balance + fee)

orderValidation: txProcessing is in apply order (classic first, then Soroban)
